paper_title: Inferred from content: Paleontological Study of Bear Premolars

methods.description: This method describes the process of compiling samples of brown bears, Deninger bears, and cave bears from various regional and chronological sources, including personal observations and literature records, and grouping them for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Compile samples of brown bears from modern, Holocene, Late Pleistocene, Eemian, and Middle/Early Pleistocene periods from specified regions/sites (Kamchatka, North America, south-eastern Europe, Near East/Africa, Alps, Spain, Central European loess area, Great Britain, Taubach, Gray's Thurrock, Atapuerca, Süssenborn, Untermaßfeld, Bad Deutsch-Altenburg).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile brown bear samples from specified geographic and chronological sources.

steps.description: Compile samples of Deninger bears from specified sites (Mosbach, Repolust cave, Cromerian Forest Bed, compilation from several sites).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile Deninger bear samples from specified geographic sources.

steps.description: Compile samples of cave bears from specified sites (Schwabenreith cave, Herdengel cave, both in Lower Austria).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile cave bear samples from specified geographic sources.

steps.description: Supplement personal observations with records from the literature to increase sample size.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate literature data with personal observations for sample compilation.

steps.description: Distinguish and group samples based on region and chronology into defined categories (e.g., Ua-A1, Ua-B1, Ud-D1, Us-C1).
steps.searchable_summary: Group compiled bear samples by region and chronology.

steps.description: Provide a detailed list of examined specimens and included references in an Appendix.

methods.description: This method describes the qualitative analysis of anterior premolars in the compiled bear samples, focusing on presence/absence, pattern within the diastema, and differences between left and right sides.

steps.description: Record the simple presence or absence of each of the three anterior premolars (P1, P2, P3) by teeth, respectively alveoli.
steps.searchable_summary: Record presence/absence of anterior premolars (P1, P2, P3) from teeth or alveoli.

steps.description: Analyze the pattern of premolars in complete diastema.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze the arrangement/pattern of premolars within the diastema.

steps.description: Observe differences between left and right halves of maxillae and mandibles, when pairs were available.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare premolar characteristics between left and right jaw halves.

steps.description: Count left and right halves of maxillae and mandibles separately for fossil samples, as they mainly consist of single halves.
steps.searchable_summary: Count left and right jaw halves separately for fossil specimens.
outputs: count of left/right halves per group

steps.description: Compare results (presence/absence, pattern, left/right differences) between the established regional and chronological groups of cave and brown bears.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare premolar analysis results across different bear groups.
